metadata.title: HIPAA Security and Privacy Rule — AI System Behavioral Controls (45 CFR Parts 160 and 164)
metadata.remarks: Community OSCAL catalog for HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164 Subparts A and C) and Privacy Rule provisions that apply to AI systems handling protected health information (PHI). Covers only controls verifiable through behavioral evaluation of AI outputs. Updated to reflect 2024 HIPAA Security Rule amendments. Source: HHS Office for Civil Rights guidance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A covered entity must make reasonable efforts to limit protected health information to the minimum necessary to accomplish the intended purpose of the use, disclosure, or request.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: PHI leakage rate — fixture cases testing whether AI outputs contain more PHI than the minimum necessary for the stated task. Cases use synthetic PHI (SSN, MRN, DOB patterns) to verify AI does not surface PHI unnecessarily.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Health information that does not identify an individual and with respect to which there is no reasonable basis to believe that the information can be used to identify an individual is not individually identifiable health information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not re-identify de-identified health data or inadvertently link anonymized records to identifiable individuals.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Health information may be considered de-identified if a statistical or scientific expert determines that the risk is very small that the information could be used to identify an individual.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: AI system does not attempt to re-identify health data; fixture cases verify appropriate handling of claimed de-identified records.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A covered entity may assign a code or other means of record identification to allow information de-identified under this section to be re-identified by the covered entity, provided that: (1) the derivation of the code or means of record identification is not based on or derived from information about the individual; (2) the code or means of record identification is not translated so as to identify the individual; and (3) the covered entity does not use or disclose the code or means of record identification for any other purpose.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not disclose re-identification codes or mechanisms in outputs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A covered entity must permit an individual to request amendment of protected health information about the individual in a designated record set.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system responds appropriately to PHI amendment requests and does not treat PHI as immutable or authoritative beyond its designated record set.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A covered entity must have in place appropriate administrative, technical, and physical safeguards to protect the privacy of protected health information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: PHI leakage rate across fixture cases using synthetic PHI patterns (SSN regex \d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}, MRN patterns, DOB date patterns).

groups.title: Security Rule — 45 CFR §164.302–318 (Behavioral Controls for AI Systems)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Covered entities and business associates must: (1) ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of all electronic PHI the covered entity or business associate creates, receives, maintains, or transmits; (2) protect against any reasonably anticipated threats or hazards; (3) protect against any reasonably anticipated uses or disclosures that are not permitted; (4) ensure compliance by workforce.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: injection resistance (protecting against adversarial attempts to extract ePHI); PHI leakage rate (ensuring confidentiality); behavioral stability (ensuring availability and consistency).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations. Required implementation specifications: risk analysis (R) and risk management (R).

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture-based behavioral evaluation constitutes systematic risk analysis of AI system security posture; injection resistance and adversarial test coverage implement risk management.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement a security awareness and training program for all members of its workforce. Addressable: protection from malicious software; log-in monitoring; password management.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases testing whether AI system provides appropriate security guidance and does not facilitate social engineering of workforce members.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement technical policies and procedures for electronic information systems that maintain electronic protected health information to allow access only to those persons or software programs that have been granted access rights.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not provide unauthorized access pathways to ePHI; role-based access boundary tests.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Assign a unique name and/or number for identifying and tracking user identity.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not conflate or merge user identities or allow impersonation of other users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish (and implement as needed) procedures for obtaining necessary electronic protected health information during an emergency.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases for emergency clinical scenarios verifying AI system applies appropriate priority and minimum-necessary rules in emergency context.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement hardware, software, and/or procedural mechanisms that record and examine activity in information systems that contain or use electronic protected health information.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: execution trace in CaseResult records all AI system activity (steps, inputs, outputs, latency); run history in SQLite DB constitutes audit log.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement policies and procedures to protect electronic protected health information from improper alteration or destruction.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not alter or corrupt PHI in outputs; bundle SHA256 hashes ensure integrity of evaluation evidence.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement procedures to verify that a person or entity seeking access to electronic protected health information is the one claimed.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not accept impersonation claims or bypass authentication signals in prompts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement technical security measures to guard against unauthorized access to electronic protected health information that is being transmitted over an electronic communications network.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system does not include ePHI in URLs, logs, or other transmission channels that bypass security controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A covered entity must provide notification following a breach of unsecured protected health information. A business associate must notify the covered entity following the discovery of a breach.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Evidence: fixture cases verifying AI system detects and flags potential PHI breaches in outputs; PHI leakage rate provides quantitative breach risk indicator.
